title: viola calaminaria subsp. westfalica
intro: Viola calaminaria subsp. westfalica, also known as Westphalian pansy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the viola genus, native to the Iserlohn district in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Viola calaminaria subsp. westfalica has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
